answer:
To authenticate with the GitHub REST API, you generally include an authentication token in the Authorization header of your request [1][2]. Use the Bearer token scheme for most cases [1][2].

```shell
curl --request GET \
--url "https://api.github.com/octocat" \
--header "Authorization: Bearer YOUR-TOKEN" \
--header "X-GitHub-Api-Version: 2026-03-10"
```

You can obtain a token via personal access tokens, GitHub Apps, or the built-in GITHUB_TOKEN in Actions workflows [1][2]. Some specific endpoints for GitHub or OAuth apps require basic authentication using the client ID as the username and client secret as the password [1][3].
